Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Request For Admissions (Set One) To Defendant David Dedia, Admitted; Request For Monetary Sanctions Of $1710.00; Declaration Of Heather A. Tovar
Off Calendar Jan-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - Plaintiffs Arkady Berger and Nelly Merzheitsky's unopposed Motion to Deem Request for Admissions (Set One) to Defendant David Dedia Admitted; Request For Monetary Sanctions Of $1,160.00 is GRANTED.

The record demonstrates Plaintiffs properly served Requests for Admission, Set One and Defendant David Dedia did not provide any responses to Plaintiffs' requests. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.280.) Defendant's failure to timely respond is not excused. No meet and confer requirement attaches when no responses have been provided.

Plaintiffs' RFAs, Set One, served on Defendant David Dedia, dated October 29, 2025, are deemed admitted.

The record does not demonstrate Defendant David Dedia acted with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Defendant Olga Jelkovsky Dedia is ordered to pay to Plaintiffs Arkady Berger and Nelly Merzheitsky $710 in sanctions, payable by February 10, 2025.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-01-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS DAVID DEDIA AND OLGA DEDIA'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.16.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED in part and only as to the activity "that supplies one or more elements of plaintiff's claims." Specifically, the allegations regarding the mechanic's lien forming the basis of the claims for breach of contract, slander of title, and UCL violation and not the filing of the bankruptcy or background facts. RGC Gaslamp LLC v. Ehmcke Sheet Metal, Co (2020) 56 Cal.App.5th 413.

Therefore, the following shall be stricken: (1) the cause of action for slander of title based in its entirety on a claim arising out of a mechanic's lien; (2) "the recordation of wrongful mechanics' lien claim against 23-23A Wood," in paragraph 43; (3) "Defendants subsequent misuse of the Mechanics' lien remedy by the subsequent recordation of a meritless Mechanics' Lien Claim, Defendants' refusal to remove their unenforceable Mechanics' lien Claims for a period of two years" in paragraph 73; (4) "by casting a cloud on title, with the recordation of meritless mechanics' lien claims" in paragraph 74.

The Court notes that Plaintiffs failed to cite any authority for the proposition that failure to remove the mechanic's lien (recordation of which Plaintiffs concede is protected activity) is legally distinguishable and is not protected/privileged. The Plaintiff further failed to show the merits of their case by establishing facts that would, if accepted, negate the litigation privilege prerequisites. Defendant's unopposed request for attorney's fees is granted in the amount of $9,280. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)
